title: Fleurya caravellana
intro: Fleurya caravellana, also known as Kalaunam or Malabar Stevia, is a perennial plant native to the Western Ghats region of India, belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. This plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation, making it a promising natural remedy for managing diabetes and other metabolic disorders. The bioactive constituents of Fleurya caravellana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, capsules, and extracts, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Fleurya caravellana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the genus Fleurya, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Lythraceae family consists of about 360 species, commonly known as the loosestrife or pryexia family.
